danger_signals.visual_form: Gunslinger character's eyes illuminate/flash on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent is about to draw and fire; player must act immediately or lose the duel

danger_signals.visual_form: Text prompt appearing above or near the gunslinger
danger_signals.meaning: Critical moment: player has window to shoot before opponent fires

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent animation showing hand moving to gun holster
danger_signals.meaning: If player has not yet fired, opponent is executing counter-attack; duel is lost if not already won

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen indicator light illuminates after premature fire
danger_signals.meaning: Player drew gun before eyes flashed; current duel is now unwinnable, must wait for next opponent
Player has been defeated; duel or game sequence has ended in loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Gunslinger sprite animation shows character falling or collapsing
safety_signals.meaning: Player successfully won the duel by shooting fast enough; immediate threat neutralized

safety_signals.visual_form: Screen change to new gunslinger character in saloon or duel setting
safety_signals.meaning: Previous duel concluded successfully; player advances to new challenge

safety_signals.visual_form: Saloon building with windows displaying stationary targets
safety_signals.meaning: Low-pressure game mode where opponents appear in windows without time pressure mechanic

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Positioned at bottom of screen, implied to be standing in saloon or desert duel setting

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Five distinct gunslinger opponents in arcade version; multiple opponents in NES version with varied sprites
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear in saloon windows or as standalone duel opponents; positioned as threats to player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gunslinger stands motionless facing player, hand near holster, neutral animated pose
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent waits; no aggressive action; player can observe before reacting

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gunslinger's eyes flash; speech bubble with 'FIRE!!' appears on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent prepares to fire; player has narrow time window to shoot first or lose

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Foul indicator light activates; gunslinger remains unharmed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player input is ignored; duel continues but is automatically lost; opponent proceeds to fire

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gunslinger sprite collapses or falls; animation shows defeat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent is eliminated; duel ends; next opponent appears

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gunslinger fires back at player; screen may show hit feedback or defeat state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Funeral march music plays; duel lost; transition to next round or game over

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Saloon building facade with multiple windows; targets appear and disappear in windows
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Targets emerge from windows briefly; no time-pressure eye-flash mechanic; player can shoot at will

environmental_storytelling.example: Game visually establishes Wild West theme through saloon building, wooden structures, and gunslinger character designs; shooting gallery mode explicitly shows saloon windows as target locations
Zapper light gun peripheral modeled after Colt Single Action Army revolver; reinforces historical Western gunfighter aesthetic and implies player is inhabiting era-appropriate gunslinger role

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Face-to-face standoff pose; opponent positioning suggests classic Western duel framing
environmental_storytelling.example: Gunslinger stance and layout communicates this is formal duel scenario rather than ambush or free-fire scenario

Five unique gunslinger characters in arcade version signal player must overcome series of increasingly difficult or varied opponents; visual differentiation creates sense of progression through 'gauntlet' of foes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stands motionless with hand near holster; maintains eye contact or neutral forward gaze
Eyes flash (visual cue); accompanied by 'FIRE!!' speech bubble; triggers player reaction window
Animation shows hand moving to revolver; if player has not fired, opponent will complete draw and fire counter-attack
Sprite collapses or falls; animation ends; opponent removed from screen
Triggers foul; gun input disabled; duel becomes unwinnable; indicates player mistimed reaction
Gun fires during eye-flash window; opponent is hit and falls; duel won; next opponent appears

notes: Wild Gunman is a reaction-time light gun game with minimal world-state complexity but highly refined danger/safety signal design. The core communication mechanism is the eye-flash + 'FIRE!!' signal pair, which creates binary tension (shoot now vs. wait). The foul mechanic adds consequence for misreading signals. Visual world-state is static (saloon/desert setting) with primary state changes expressed through opponent animation and audio cues. No faction politics or territorial control; conflict is purely personal duel-based. The game's visual language prioritizes clarity of the single decision point (when to shoot) over environmental narrative.
